title: Mentzelia procera
taxonomy: Mentzelia procera is classified in the family Loasaceae, commonly known as the incense tree or wild tobacco family. This family consists of about 80 species of flowering plants. Mentzelia procera is a genus of flowering plants that are native to western North America. The Loasaceae family is part of the order Cornales, which also includes families such as Cornaceae and Hydrangeaceae. The classification of Mentzelia procera reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.

intro: Mentzelia procera, also known as the tall blazing star, is a perennial plant that belongs to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
